summary: Russian forces launched a large-scale combined missile and drone attack across Ukraine, deploying 142 strike drones, Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles from multiple directions, with 132 targets reportedly intercepted. Ground offensives continued in Donetsk region with Russian MoD claiming capture of settlements in Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts. A separate drone strike caused oil reservoir fires in Krasnodar Krai, Russia, while China and Russia conducted joint air patrols near South Korea's ADIZ.
key_points: Russian forces deployed 142 strike drones, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and two Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles in a massive overnight attack on June 27-28, with air defenses claiming 132 targets shot down or suppressed. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Russian MoD claimed capture of Pisansy in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and Novoselivka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, while also claiming destruction of Ukrainian strongpoints in Zaporizhzhia region. (Source: @tass_agency/382744, @tass_agency/382721)
Drone attack struck oil reservoirs in Krasnodar Krai, Russia, with fires reported, likely in Slavyansk-on-Kuban area. (Source: @eRadarrua/98447, @tass_agency/382714)

Ukraine's air defenses faced ballistic missile threats toward Kyiv, Chuhuyiv (Kharkiv Oblast), and multiple Shahed drone waves targeting northern, southern, and eastern regions throughout the night. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68117, @mon1tor_ua/82238, @kpszsu/66637)

China and Russia conducted joint air patrol over the Sea of Japan, East China Sea, and western Pacific, with nearly ten aircraft entering South Korea's air defense identification zone, prompting Seoul to scramble fighters. (Source: @UkraineNow/82228)

Ukrainian officials reported approximately 16,000 Ukrainian civilians may be held captive in Russia, with Ukraine confirming 1,878 cases; the Ombudsman accused Russia of reclassifying civilians as POWs to inflate exchange funds. (Source: @UkraineNow/82224)

Russian forces launched over 40 attacks on two districts of Dnipropetrovsk Oblast during the day, killing one man, wounding another, damaging gas stations, vehicles, and igniting fires at a private residence and wheat field. (Source: @UkraineNow/82229)
Ukrainian forces struck Naftogaz facilities in Poltava and Kharkiv regions over the past two days with missile and drone strikes. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31211)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces executed a large-scale combined arms overnight attack deploying 142 strike drones alongside ballistic and anti-ship missiles from multiple launch sites across Russia and occupied territories, with Ukrainian air defenses claiming 132 of 132+ targets intercepted. Ground operations intensified as Russian MoD claimed territorial gains in Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts while continuing the Donetsk offensive toward the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration. Simultaneously, Ukrainian drone strikes targeted Russian oil infrastructure in Krasnodar Krai causing fires, and China-Russia joint air patrols triggered South Korean fighter scrambles near the ADIZ.

points: Ukrainian air defenses reported intercepting 132 of 132+ incoming targets during the June 27-28 overnight assault, including one Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missile, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 125 strike drones of types Shahed, Gerber, Italmas, and Parody decoys. Strikes hit 11 locations with falling debris impacting 13 additional sites. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Ukrainian forces struck Naftogaz gas processing facilities in Poltava and Kharkiv regions over a two-day period using missiles and drones, targeting Russian energy infrastructure. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31211)

Drone strikes targeted oil reservoirs in Krasnodar Krai, Russia, with fires reported; one strike attributed to falling drone debris hitting an oil refinery. (Source: @eRadarrua/98447, @tass_agency/382714)

Ukrainian officials reported approximately 16,000 Ukrainian civilians may be held in Russian captivity, with 1,878 cases officially confirmed; the Parliamentary Commissioner for Human Rights stated Russia is reclassifying civilians as POWs to artificially inflate its prisoner exchange fund. (Source: @UkraineNow/82224)

Ukrainian authorities reported Russian forces attacked Dnipropetrovsk Oblast over 40 times in one day using drones and artillery, destroying three gas stations, igniting a wheat field fire, and killing one civilian with one wounded. (Source: @UkraineNow/82229)

Ukrainian officials reported Russian KAB guided bombs struck Zaporizhzhia Oblast center, wounding three people including a child, with damage to medical and educational facilities and private homes. (Source: @UkraineNow/82238, @UkraineNow/82236)
Ukrainian officials reported nearly 20 drone attacks on Dnipropetrovsk Oblast damaged three gas stations, ignited a sports school, and wounded two people. (Source: @UkraineNow/82233)
Russian MoD claimed Armed Forces liberated Pisansy in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and Novoselivka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382744)
Russian MoD claimed forces foiled a Ukrainian UAV attack and destroyed Ukrainian strongpoints in Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382721)
Russian MoD claimed forces in northwestern Krasny Lyman captured 5 strongpoints and 59 buildings. (Source: @tass_agency/382746)

Russian MoD reported mortar crews supporting successful assault operations in Konstantinovka, Donetsk region, with the 1st Battalion of the 1465th Motor Rifle Regiment, 4th Brigade of the Southern Group of Forces cited. (Source: @tass_agency/382738)

Russian forces launched a massive combined strike on the night of June 27-28 using two Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles from Kursk Oblast, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles from Bryansk Oblast, and 142 strike drones from directions including Kursk, Oryol, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, Donetsk, and Hvardiyske (Crimea). (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Russian tactical aviation employed KAB guided bombs against Zaporizhzhia Oblast, Donetsk region, and Kharkiv Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66649, @kpszsu/66638)
Russian forces launched over 40 artillery and drone attacks on two districts of Dnipropetrovsk Oblast within a single day. (Source: @UkraineNow/82229)

Russian forces continued offensive operations toward the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration in Donetsk region, with the assault on Konstantinovka described as a heavy months-long effort, while working to isolate and encircle Krasny Lyman. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)
Russian MoD claimed capture of Pisansy in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, representing continued eastward territorial gains beyond the Donetsk administrative boundary. (Source: @tass_agency/382744)
Russian forces deployed KAB guided bombs against eastern Kharkiv Oblast, with ballistic missile threats reported toward Chuhuyiv. (Source: @kpszsu/66638, @mon1tor_ua/68137, @mon1tor_ua/68136)
Ukrainian officials reported Russian forces struck Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv region with missiles and drones over a two-day period. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31211)

Bloomberg reported Ukrainian positions in Donetsk region continue to deteriorate with Russian forces moving closer to Kramatorsk and Slavyansk; military analyst Michael Kofman noted the fight for Donetsk region continues despite media attention on Ukrainian rear-area strikes. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214)

Russian MoD claimed mortar crews from the 1465th Motor Rifle Regiment's 1st Battalion, 4th Brigade of Southern Group of Forces, supported successful assault operations in Konstantinovka. (Source: @tass_agency/382738)

Multiple Shahed drone waves were tracked from Kharkiv Oblast into Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and toward Dnipro, with additional reactive-type drones crossing Chernihiv Oblast toward Kyiv Oblast (Vyshgorod district) and Zhytomyr Oblast toward Korosten. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68113, @kpszsu/66626, @kpszsu/66628)

Ballistic missile threats were reported toward Kyiv from northeastern and northern directions, with air raid alerts active in the capital region overnight. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68117, @mon1tor_ua/68118, @mon1tor_ua/68120, @mon1tor_ua/68143)

Ukrainian officials reported a massive drone attack on Chernihiv Oblast, wounding three people including a 48-year-old woman and 72-year-old man with shrapnel injuries, damaging medical and educational facilities, private homes, and vehicles. (Source: @UkraineNow/82235)
Reactive-type drones were tracked along the Chernihiv Oblast border toward Slavutych, with additional drones in Sumy Oblast near Konotop and Sumy. (Source: @eRadarrua/98475, @kpszsu/66609)
Ukrainian air defenses reported ballistic missile threat from the north on June 27, with air defense operations ongoing across northern regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66659, @eRadarrua/98469)
Russian MoD claimed capture of Novoselivka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast and destruction of Ukrainian strongpoints in the region. (Source: @tass_agency/382744, @tass_agency/382721)
Russian KAB guided bomb strikes targeted Zaporizhzhia Oblast, with the regional center hit and three wounded including a child. (Source: @kpszsu/66649, @UkraineNow/82238, @UkraineNow/82236)
Drones were tracked toward Zaporizhzhia from the south, with two reactive-type drones passing Nikopol toward Sofiyivka. (Source: @kpszsu/66636, @eRadarrua/98492)
Ukrainian officials reported a drone on Kamianske reservoir approaching Kremenchuk, with another south of Reshetilivka in Poltava Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98500)
Drones tracked toward Voznesensk in Mykolaiv Oblast, with additional drones on the border of Sumy and Chernihiv oblasts heading south. (Source: @kpszsu/66638, @eRadarrua/98475)
Ukrainian forces struck Naftogaz facilities in Poltava region, with drones crossing from Dnipropetrovsk Oblast toward Kirovohrad Oblast. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31211, @kpszsu/66626)

The June 27-28 overnight assault represented one of the largest combined strikes, with 142 strike drones (including Shahed, reactive Shahed, Gerber, Italmas, and Parody decoy types), two Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles from Kursk Oblast, and six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles from Bryansk Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Ukrainian Defense Forces reported shooting down or suppressing 132 targets: one Oniks/Zircon, six Iskander-M/S-400, and 125 enemy drones of various types across northern, southern, and eastern regions. Impacts recorded at 11 locations with debris falling at 13 additional sites. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Russian strategic aviation Tu-95MS bombers were reported preparing for redeployment from Ukrainka airfield for a potential major strike on Kyiv involving ballistic, cruise, hypersonic missiles and strike drones, though the attack was assessed as not imminent. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111)
Russian tactical aviation conducted KAB guided bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia Oblast, Donetsk region, and Kharkiv Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66649, @kpszsu/66638)

Ukrainian drone strikes caused fires at oil reservoirs in Krasnodar Krai, Russia, with one incident involving falling drone debris igniting an oil refinery. (Source: @eRadarrua/98447, @tass_agency/382714)

China and Russia conducted joint air patrol with nearly ten aircraft over the Sea of Japan, East China Sea, and western Pacific, entering South Korea's ADIZ and prompting Seoul to scramble fighter jets. (Source: @UkraineNow/82228)

key_developments: Russian forces launched 142 strike drones, two Oniks/Zircon missiles, and six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles in a massive overnight combined attack on June 27-28 from launch sites including Kursk, Oryol, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, Donetsk, and Crimea. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Ukrainian air defenses claimed 132 targets intercepted during the overnight assault, with impacts at 11 locations and debris at 13 additional sites across the country. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Russian MoD claimed territorial gains with capture of Pisansy (Dnipropetrovsk Oblast) and Novoselivka (Zaporizhzhia Oblast), plus 5 strongpoints and 59 buildings in northwestern Krasny Lyman. (Source: @tass_agency/382744, @tass_agency/382746)
Ukrainian drone strikes caused fires at oil reservoirs in Krasnodar Krai, Russia, with one oil refinery reportedly ignited by falling drone debris. (Source: @eRadarrua/98447, @tass_agency/382714)
China-Russia joint air patrol with nearly ten aircraft entered South Korea's ADIZ over the Sea of Japan and East China Sea, triggering South Korean fighter scrambles. (Source: @UkraineNow/82228)

Ukrainian officials reported approximately 16,000 civilians may be held captive in Russia, with accusations that Moscow reclassifies civilians as POWs to inflate exchange funds. (Source: @UkraineNow/82224)
Russian forces attacked Dnipropetrovsk Oblast over 40 times in one day, destroying three gas stations, igniting a wheat field, and killing one civilian. (Source: @UkraineNow/82229)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is escalating significantly on both sides. Russia's large-scale combined missile and drone assault on June 27-28 demonstrates continued capacity for complex multi-axis strikes using cruise, ballistic, and anti-ship missiles alongside massed drone swarms from launch sites spanning Kursk to Crimea. The redeployment of Tu-95MS strategic bombers to Ukrainka signals preparation for potentially larger strikes on Kyiv. Simultaneously, Russia's ground offensive in Donetsk region continues pushing toward the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration, with Ukrainian positions described as deteriorating. Ukraine's ability to strike Russian energy infrastructure in Krasnodar Krai and Poltava/Kharkiv regions demonstrates expanding rear-area strike capability, though the intensity of Russian strikes on civilian infrastructure across multiple oblasts indicates Ukraine's air defense network remains under severe strain across all axes.
sections.regional_response: Finland's President Alexander Stubb stated that neither sanctions nor Russian military losses will end the war, identifying only domestic Russian public unrest as a potential factor that could compel the Kremlin to stop. China and Russia conducted joint air patrols through South Korea's ADIZ, with Beijing framing the operation as demonstrating joint resolve to ensure regional stability. The EU is reportedly shifting defense procurement toward mass production of drones, missiles, and guided bombs as cheaper means of achieving disarmament strike capability against Russia. Ukraine's Ombudsman raised the civilian hostage issue internationally, noting that freed Ukrainian POWs provide testimony confirming the presence of civilians in Russian detention facilities.
sections.intelligence_notes: The June 27-28 air attack is notable for the inclusion of Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles in a land-attack role against Kyiv from Kursk Oblast, suggesting either repurposing of naval assets or cross-domain deployment. The use of Parody-type decoy drones alongside Shahed and Gerber systems indicates Russian adaptation to saturate Ukrainian air defense discrimination capabilities. The reported 132 interceptions out of 132+ claimed targets requires independent verification given the 11 impact locations reported. The concentration of Russian forces visible for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk battle, combined with civilian evacuations from the agglomeration, suggests a major offensive operation is imminent in Donetsk region. The Krasnodar Krai oil infrastructure strikes represent continued Ukrainian capability to project into Russian territory, which may prompt enhanced Russian air defense deployments to southern rear areas.
